:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       SFTP   curl supports SFTP (draft 5) done over SSH version 2.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       SMB(S) curl supports SMB version 1 for upload and download.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       SMTP(S)
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Uploading contents to an SMTP server means sending an email. With or without TLS.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       TELNET Telling curl to fetch a telnet URL starts an interactive session where it sends what it reads on stdin and outputs what the server sends it.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       TFTP   curl can do TFTP downloads and uploads.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K[1mPROGRESS[0m [1mMETER[0m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       curl normally displays a progress meter during operations, indicating the amount of transferred data, transfer speeds and estimated time left, etc. The progress meter displays the transfer rate
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       in bytes per second. The suffixes (k, M, G, T, P) are 1024 based. For example 1k is 1024 bytes. 1M is 1048576 bytes.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       curl displays this data to the terminal by default, so if you invoke curl to do an operation and it is about to write data to the terminal, it [4mdisables[24m the progress meter as otherwise it would
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       mess up the output mixing progress meter and response data.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       If you want a progress meter for HTTP POST or PUT requests, you need to redirect the response output to a file, using shell redirect (>), [4m-o,[24m [4m--output[24m or similar.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       This does not apply to FTP upload as that operation does not spit out any response data to the terminal.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       If you prefer a progress "bar" instead of the regular meter, [4m-#,[24m [4m--progress-bar[24m is your friend. You can also disable the progress meter completely with the [4m-s,[24m [4m--silent[24m option.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K[1mVERSION[0m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       This man page describes curl %VERSION. If you use a later version, chances are this man page does not fully document it. If you use an earlier version, this document tries to include version
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       information about which specific version that introduced changes.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       You can always learn which the latest curl version is by running
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       curl https://curl.se/info
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       The online version of this man page is always showing the latest incarnation: https://curl.se/docs/manpage.html
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K[1mOPTIONS[0m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       Options start with one or two dashes. Many of the options require an additional value next to them. If provided text does not start with a dash, it is presumed to be and treated as a URL.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       The short "single-dash" form of the options, -d for example, may be used with or without a space between it and its value, although a space is a recommended separator. The long "double-dash"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       form, [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m for example, requires a space between it and its value.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       Short version options that do not need any additional values can be used immediately next to each other, like for example you can specify all the options [4m-O[24m, [4m-L[24m and [4m-v[24m at once as [4m-OLv[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       In general, all boolean options are enabled with --[1moption[0m and yet again disabled with --[1mno-[0moption. That is, you use the same option name but prefix it with "no-". However, in this list we
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       mostly only list and show the [4m--option[24m version of them.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       When [4m-:,[24m [4m--next[24m is used, it resets the parser state and you start again with a clean option state, except for the options that are "global". Global options retain their values and meaning even
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       after [4m-:,[24m [4m--next[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       The following options are global: [4m--fail-early[24m, [4m--libcurl[24m, [4m--parallel-immediate[24m, [4m-Z,[24m [4m--parallel[24m, [4m-#,[24m [4m--progress-bar[24m, [4m--rate[24m, [4m-S,[24m [4m--show-error[24m, [4m--stderr[24m, [4m--styled-output[24m, [4m--trace-ascii[24m,
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       [4m--trace-config[24m, [4m--trace-ids[24m, [4m--trace-time[24m, [4m--trace[24m and [4m-v,[24m [4m--verbose[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --abstract-unix-socket <path>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Connect through an abstract Unix domain socket, instead of using the network.  Note: netstat shows the path of an abstract socket prefixed with '@', however the <path> argument
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              should not have this leading character.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --abstract-unix-socket is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --abstract-unix-socket socketpath https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--unix-socket[24m. Added in 7.53.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --alt-svc <file name>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTPS) This option enables the alt-svc parser in curl. If the file name points to an existing alt-svc cache file, that gets used. After a completed transfer, the cache is saved to the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              file name again if it has been modified.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Specify a "" file name (zero length) to avoid loading/saving and make curl just handle the cache in memory.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If this option is used several times, curl loads contents from all the files but the last one is used for saving.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --alt-svc can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --alt-svc svc.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--resolve[24m and [4m--connect-to[24m. Added in 7.64.1.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --anyauth
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Tells curl to figure out authentication method by itself, and use the most secure one the remote site claims to support. This is done by first doing a request and checking the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              response-headers, thus possibly inducing an extra network round-trip. This is used instead of setting a specific authentication method, which you can do with [4m--basic[24m, [4m--digest[24m, [4m--ntlm[24m,
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              and [4m--negotiate[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Using [4m--anyauth[24m is not recommended if you do uploads from stdin, since it may require data to be sent twice and then the client must be able to rewind. If the need should arise when
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              uploading from stdin, the upload operation fails.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Used together with [4m-u,[24m [4m--user[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --anyauth multiple times has no extra effect.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --anyauth --user me:pwd https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--proxy-anyauth[24m, [4m--basic[24m and [4m--digest[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -a, --append
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP SFTP) When used in an upload, this option makes curl append to the target file instead of overwriting it. If the remote file does not exist, it is created. Note that this flag is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              ignored by some SFTP servers (including OpenSSH).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --append multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-append.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --upload-file local --append ftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-r,[24m [4m--range[24m and [4m-C,[24m [4m--continue-at[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --aws-sigv4 <provider1[:provider2[:region[:service]]]>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Use AWS V4 signature authentication in the transfer.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The provider argument is a string that is used by the algorithm when creating outgoing authentication headers.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The region argument is a string that points to a geographic area of a resources collection (region-code) when the region name is omitted from the endpoint.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The service argument is a string that points to a function provided by a cloud (service-code) when the service name is omitted from the endpoint.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --aws-sigv4 is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --aws-sigv4 "aws:amz:us-east-2:es" --user "key:secret" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--basic[24m and [4m-u,[24m [4m--user[24m. Added in 7.75.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --basic
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Tells curl to use HTTP Basic authentication with the remote host. This is the default and this option is usually pointless, unless you use it to override a previously set option
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              that sets a different authentication method (such as [4m--ntlm[24m, [4m--digest[24m, or [4m--negotiate[24m).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Used together with [4m-u,[24m [4m--user[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --basic multiple times has no extra effect.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -u name:password --basic https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--proxy-basic[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ca-native
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Tells curl to use the CA store from the native operating system to verify the peer. By default, curl otherwise uses a CA store provided in a single file or directory, but when
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              using this option it interfaces the operating system's own vault.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option works for curl on Windows when built to use OpenSSL, wolfSSL (added in 8.3.0) or GnuTLS (added in 8.5.0). When curl on Windows is built to use Schannel, this feature is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              implied and curl then only uses the native CA store.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ca-native multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-ca-native.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ca-native https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--cacert[24m, [4m--capath[24m and [4m-k,[24m [4m--insecure[24m. Added in 8.2.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --cacert <file>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Tells curl to use the specified certificate file to verify the peer. The file may contain multiple CA certificates. The certificate(s) must be in PEM format. Normally curl is built
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              to use a default file for this, so this option is typically used to alter that default file.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl recognizes the environment variable named 'CURL_CA_BUNDLE' if it is set and the TLS backend is not Schannel, and uses the given path as a path to a CA cert bundle. This option
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              overrides that variable.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The windows version of curl automatically looks for a CA certs file named 'curl-ca-bundle.crt', either in the same directory as curl.exe, or in the Current Working Directory, or in any
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              folder along your PATH.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (iOS and macOS only) If curl is built against Secure Transport, then this option is supported for backward compatibility with other SSL engines, but it should not be set. If the option
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              is not set, then curl uses the certificates in the system and user Keychain to verify the peer, which is the preferred method of verifying the peer's certificate chain.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (Schannel only) This option is supported for Schannel in Windows 7 or later (added in 7.60.0). This option is supported for backward compatibility with other SSL engines; instead it is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              recommended to use Windows' store of root certificates (the default for Schannel).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --cacert is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --cacert CA-file.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--capath[24m and [4m-k,[24m [4m--insecure[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --capath <dir>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Tells curl to use the specified certificate directory to verify the peer. Multiple paths can be provided by separating them with ":" (e.g.  "path1:path2:path3"). The certificates
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              must be in PEM format, and if curl is built against OpenSSL, the directory must have been processed using the c_rehash utility supplied with OpenSSL. Using [4m--capath[24m can allow
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              OpenSSL-powered curl to make SSL-connections much more efficiently than using [4m--cacert[24m if the [4m--cacert[24m file contains many CA certificates.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If this option is set, the default capath value is ignored.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --capath is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --capath /local/directory https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--cacert[24m and [4m-k,[24m [4m--insecure[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --cert-status
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Tells curl to verify the status of the server certificate by using the Certificate Status Request (aka. OCSP stapling) TLS extension.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If this option is enabled and the server sends an invalid (e.g. expired) response, if the response suggests that the server certificate has been revoked, or no response at all is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              received, the verification fails.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This is currently only implemented in the OpenSSL and GnuTLS backends.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --cert-status multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-cert-status.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --cert-status https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--pinnedpubkey[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --cert-type <type>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Tells curl what type the provided client certificate is using. PEM, DER, ENG and P12 are recognized types.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The default type depends on the TLS backend and is usually PEM, however for Secure Transport and Schannel it is P12. If [4m-E,[24m [4m--cert[24m is a pkcs11: URI then ENG is the default type.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --cert-type is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --cert-type PEM --cert file https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-E,[24m [4m--cert[24m, [4m--key[24m and [4m--key-type[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -E, --cert <certificate[:password]>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Tells curl to use the specified client certificate file when getting a file with HTTPS, FTPS or another SSL-based protocol. The certificate must be in PKCS#12 format if using
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Secure Transport, or PEM format if using any other engine. If the optional password is not specified, it is queried for on the terminal. Note that this option assumes a certificate file
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              that is the private key and the client certificate concatenated. See [4m-E,[24m [4m--cert[24m and [4m--key[24m to specify them independently.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              In the <certificate> portion of the argument, you must escape the character ":" as "\:" so that it is not recognized as the password delimiter. Similarly, you must escape the double
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              quote character as \" so that it is not recognized as an escape character.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If curl is built against OpenSSL library, and the engine pkcs11 is available, then a PKCS#11 URI (RFC 7512) can be used to specify a certificate located in a PKCS#11 device. A string
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              beginning with "pkcs11:" is interpreted as a PKCS#11 URI. If a PKCS#11 URI is provided, then the [4m--engine[24m option is set as "pkcs11" if none was provided and the [4m--cert-type[24m option is set
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              as "ENG" if none was provided.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (iOS and macOS only) If curl is built against Secure Transport, then the certificate string can either be the name of a certificate/private key in the system or user keychain, or the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              path to a PKCS#12-encoded certificate and private key. If you want to use a file from the current directory, please precede it with "./" prefix, in order to avoid confusion with a
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              nickname.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (Schannel only) Client certificates must be specified by a path expression to a certificate store. (Loading [4mPFX[24m is not supported; you can import it to a store first). You can use "<store
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              location>\<store name>\<thumbprint>" to refer to a certificate in the system certificates store, for example, [4m"CurrentUser\MY\934a7ac6f8a5d579285a74fa61e19f23ddfe8d7a"[24m. Thumbprint is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              usually a SHA-1 hex string which you can see in certificate details. Following store locations are supported: [4mCurrentUser[24m, [4mLocalMachine[24m, [4mCurrentService[24m, [4mServices[24m, [4mCurrentUserGroupPolicy[24m,
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [4mLocalMachineGroupPolicy[24m and [4mLocalMachineEnterprise[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --cert is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --cert certfile --key keyfile https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--cert-type[24m, [4m--key[24m and [4m--key-type[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ciphers <list of ciphers>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Specifies which ciphers to use in the connection. The list of ciphers must specify valid ciphers. Read up on SSL cipher list details on this URL:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              https://curl.se/docs/ssl-ciphers.html
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --ciphers is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ciphers ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-CCM8 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--tlsv1.3[24m, [4m--tls13-ciphers[24m and [4m--proxy-ciphers[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --compressed-ssh
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (SCP SFTP) Enables built-in SSH compression.  This is a request, not an order; the server may or may not do it.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --compressed-ssh multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-compressed-ssh.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --compressed-ssh sftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--compressed[24m. Added in 7.56.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --compressed
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Request a compressed response using one of the algorithms curl supports, and automatically decompress the content.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Response headers are not modified when saved, so if they are "interpreted" separately again at a later point they might appear to be saying that the content is (still) compressed; while
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              in fact it has already been decompressed.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If this option is used and the server sends an unsupported encoding, curl reports an error. This is a request, not an order; the server may or may not deliver data compressed.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --compressed multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-compressed.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --compressed https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--compressed-ssh[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -K, --config <file>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Specify a text file to read curl arguments from. The command line arguments found in the text file are used as if they were provided on the command line.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Options and their parameters must be specified on the same line in the file, separated by whitespace, colon, or the equals sign. Long option names can optionally be given in the config
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              file without the initial double dashes and if so, the colon or equals characters can be used as separators. If the option is specified with one or two dashes, there can be no colon or
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              equals character between the option and its parameter.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If the parameter contains whitespace or starts with a colon (:) or equals sign (=), it must be specified enclosed within double quotes ("). Within double quotes the following escape
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              sequences are available: \\, \", \t, \n, \r and \v. A backslash preceding any other letter is ignored.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If the first non-blank column of a config line is a '#' character, that line is treated as a comment.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Only write one option per physical line in the config file. A single line is required to be no more than 10 megabytes (since 8.2.0).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Specify the filename to [4m-K,[24m [4m--config[24m as '-' to make curl read the file from stdin.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Note that to be able to specify a URL in the config file, you need to specify it using the [4m--url[24m option, and not by simply writing the URL on its own line. So, it could look similar to
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              this:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              url = "https://curl.se/docs/"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              # --- Example file ---
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              # this is a comment
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              url = "example.com"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              output = "curlhere.html"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              user-agent = "superagent/1.0"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              # and fetch another URL too
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              url = "example.com/docs/manpage.html"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              -O
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              referer = "http://nowhereatall.example.com/"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              # --- End of example file ---
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              When curl is invoked, it (unless [4m-q,[24m [4m--disable[24m is used) checks for a default config file and uses it if found, even when [4m-K,[24m [4m--config[24m is used. The default config file is checked for in
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              the following places in this order:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              1) [1m"$CURL_HOME/.curlrc"[0m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              2) [1m"$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/curlrc"[0m (Added in 7.73.0)
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              3) [1m"$HOME/.curlrc"[0m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              4) Windows: [1m"%USERPROFILE%\.curlrc"[0m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              5) Windows: [1m"%APPDATA%\.curlrc"[0m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              6) Windows: [1m"%USERPROFILE%\Application[0m [1mData\.curlrc"[0m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              7) Non-Windows: use getpwuid to find the home directory
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              8) On Windows, if it finds no [4m.curlrc[24m file in the sequence described above, it checks for one in the same dir the curl executable is placed.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              On Windows two filenames are checked per location: [4m.curlrc[24m and [4m_curlrc[24m, preferring the former. Older versions on Windows checked for [4m_curlrc[24m only.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --config can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --config file.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-q,[24m [4m--disable[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --connect-timeout <fractional seconds>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Maximum time in seconds that you allow curl's connection to take. This only limits the connection phase, so if curl connects within the given period it continues - if not it exits.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option accepts decimal values. The decimal value needs to be provided using a dot (.) as decimal separator - not the local version even if it might be using another separator.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The connection phase is considered complete when the DNS lookup and requested TCP, TLS or QUIC handshakes are done.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --connect-timeout is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --connect-timeout 20 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --connect-timeout 3.14 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-m,[24m [4m--max-time[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --connect-to <HOST1:PORT1:HOST2:PORT2>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For a request to the given "HOST1:PORT1" pair, connect to "HOST2:PORT2" instead. This option is suitable to direct requests at a specific server, e.g. at a specific cluster node in a
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              cluster of servers. This option is only used to establish the network connection. It does NOT affect the hostname/port that is used for TLS/SSL (e.g. SNI, certificate verification) or
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              for the application protocols. "HOST1" and "PORT1" may be the empty string, meaning "any host/port". "HOST2" and "PORT2" may also be the empty string, meaning "use the request's original
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              host/port".
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              A hostname specified to this option is compared as a string, so it needs to match the name used in request URL. It can be either numerical such as "127.0.0.1" or the full host name such
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              as "example.org".
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --connect-to can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --connect-to example.com:443:example.net:8443 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--resolve[24m and [4m-H,[24m [4m--header[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -C, --continue-at <offset>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Continue/Resume a previous file transfer at the given offset. The given offset is the exact number of bytes that are skipped, counting from the beginning of the source file before it is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              transferred to the destination. If used with uploads, the FTP server command SIZE is not used by curl.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Use "-C -" to tell curl to automatically find out where/how to resume the transfer. It then uses the given output/input files to figure that out.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --continue-at is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -C - https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -C 400 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-r,[24m [4m--range[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -c, --cookie-jar <filename>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Specify to which file you want curl to write all cookies after a completed operation. Curl writes all cookies from its in-memory cookie storage to the given file at the end of
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              operations. If no cookies are known, no data is written. The file is created using the Netscape cookie file format. If you set the file name to a single dash, "-", the cookies are
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              written to stdout.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The file specified with [4m-c,[24m [4m--cookie-jar[24m is only used for output. No cookies are read from the file. To read cookies, use the [4m-b,[24m [4m--cookie[24m option. Both options can specify the same file.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This command line option activates the cookie engine that makes curl record and use cookies. The [4m-b,[24m [4m--cookie[24m option also activates it.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If the cookie jar cannot be created or written to, the whole curl operation does not fail or even report an error clearly. Using [4m-v,[24m [4m--verbose[24m gets a warning displayed, but that is the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              only visible feedback you get about this possibly lethal situation.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --cookie-jar is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -c store-here.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -c store-here.txt -b read-these https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-b,[24m [4m--cookie[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -b, --cookie <data|filename>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Pass the data to the HTTP server in the Cookie header. It is supposedly the data previously received from the server in a "Set-Cookie:" line. The data should be in the format
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              "NAME1=VALUE1; NAME2=VALUE2". This makes curl use the cookie header with this content explicitly in all outgoing request(s). If multiple requests are done due to authentication, followed
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              redirects or similar, they all get this cookie passed on.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If no '=' symbol is used in the argument, it is instead treated as a filename to read previously stored cookie from. This option also activates the cookie engine which makes curl record
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              incoming cookies, which may be handy if you are using this in combination with the [4m-L,[24m [4m--location[24m option or do multiple URL transfers on the same invoke.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If the file name is exactly a minus ("-"), curl instead reads the contents from stdin. If the file name is an empty string ("") and is the only cookie input, curl will activate the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              cookie engine without any cookies.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The file format of the file to read cookies from should be plain HTTP headers (Set-Cookie style) or the Netscape/Mozilla cookie file format.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The file specified with [4m-b,[24m [4m--cookie[24m is only used as input. No cookies are written to the file. To store cookies, use the [4m-c,[24m [4m--cookie-jar[24m option.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If you use the Set-Cookie file format and do not specify a domain then the cookie is not sent since the domain never matches. To address this, set a domain in Set-Cookie line (doing that
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              includes subdomains) or preferably: use the Netscape format.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Users often want to both read cookies from a file and write updated cookies back to a file, so using both [4m-b,[24m [4m--cookie[24m and [4m-c,[24m [4m--cookie-jar[24m in the same command line is common.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If curl is built with PSL ([1mPublic[0m [1mSuffix[0m [1mList[0m) support, it detects and discards cookies that are specified for such suffix domains that should not be allowed to have cookies. If curl is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [4mnot[24m built with PSL support, it has no ability to stop super cookies.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --cookie can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -b "" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -b cookiefile https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -b cookiefile -c cookiefile https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-c,[24m [4m--cookie-jar[24m and [4m-j,[24m [4m--junk-session-cookies[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --create-dirs
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              When used in conjunction with the [4m-o,[24m [4m--output[24m option, curl creates the necessary local directory hierarchy as needed. This option creates the directories mentioned with the [4m-o,[24m [4m--output[24m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              option combined with the path possibly set with [4m--output-dir[24m. If the combined output file name uses no directory, or if the directories it mentions already exist, no directories are
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              created.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Created directories are made with mode 0750 on unix style file systems.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              To create remote directories when using FTP or SFTP, try [4m--ftp-create-dirs[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --create-dirs multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-create-dirs.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --create-dirs --output local/dir/file https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ftp-create-dirs[24m and [4m--output-dir[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --create-file-mode <mode>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (SFTP SCP FILE) When curl is used to create files remotely using one of the supported protocols, this option allows the user to set which 'mode' to set on the file at creation time,
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              instead of the default 0644.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option takes an octal number as argument.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --create-file-mode is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --create-file-mode 0777 -T localfile sftp://example.com/new
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ftp-create-dirs[24m. Added in 7.75.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --crlf (FTP SMTP) Convert line feeds to carriage return plus line feeds in upload. Useful for [1mMVS[0m [1m(OS/390)[0m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (SMTP added in 7.40.0)
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --crlf multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-crlf.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --crlf -T file ftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-B,[24m [4m--use-ascii[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --crlfile <file>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Provide a file using PEM format with a Certificate Revocation List that may specify peer certificates that are to be considered revoked.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --crlfile is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --crlfile rejects.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--cacert[24m and [4m--capath[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --curves <algorithm list>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Tells curl to request specific curves to use during SSL session establishment according to RFC 8422, 5.1. Multiple algorithms can be provided by separating them with ":" (e.g.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              "X25519:P-521"). The parameter is available identically in the OpenSSL "s_client" and "s_server" utilities.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [4m--curves[24m allows a OpenSSL powered curl to make SSL-connections with exactly the (EC) curve requested by the client, avoiding nontransparent client/server negotiations.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If this option is set, the default curves list built into OpenSSL are ignored.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --curves is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --curves X25519 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ciphers[24m. Added in 7.73.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --data-ascii <data>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) This is just an alias for [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --data-ascii can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --data-ascii @file https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--data-binary[24m, [4m--data-raw[24m and [4m--data-urlencode[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --data-binary <data>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) This posts data exactly as specified with no extra processing whatsoever.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If you start the data with the letter @, the rest should be a filename. Data is posted in a similar manner as [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m does, except that newlines and carriage returns are preserved and
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              conversions are never done.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Like [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m the default content-type sent to the server is application/x-www-form-urlencoded. If you want the data to be treated as arbitrary binary data by the server then set the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              content-type to octet-stream: -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream".
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If this option is used several times, the ones following the first append data as described in [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --data-binary can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --data-binary @filename https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--data-ascii[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --data-raw <data>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) This posts data similarly to [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m but without the special interpretation of the @ character.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --data-raw can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --data-raw "hello" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --data-raw "@at@at@" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --data-urlencode <data>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) This posts data, similar to the other [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m options with the exception that this performs URL-encoding.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              To be CGI-compliant, the <data> part should begin with a [4mname[24m followed by a separator and a content specification. The <data> part can be passed to curl using one of the following
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              syntaxes:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              content
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     This makes curl URL-encode the content and pass that on. Just be careful so that the content does not contain any = or @ symbols, as that makes the syntax match one of the other
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     cases below!
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              =content
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     This makes curl URL-encode the content and pass that on. The preceding = symbol is not included in the data.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              name=content
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     This makes curl URL-encode the content part and pass that on. Note that the name part is expected to be URL-encoded already.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              @filename
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     This makes curl load data from the given file (including any newlines), URL-encode that data and pass it on in the POST.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              name@filename
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     This makes curl load data from the given file (including any newlines), URL-encode that data and pass it on in the POST. The name part gets an equal sign appended, resulting in
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     [4mname=urlencoded-file-content[24m. Note that the name is expected to be URL-encoded already.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --data-urlencode can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --data-urlencode name=val https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --data-urlencode =encodethis https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --data-urlencode name@file https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --data-urlencode @fileonly https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m and [4m--data-raw[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -d, --data <data>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP MQTT) Sends the specified data in a POST request to the HTTP server, in the same way that a browser does when a user has filled in an HTML form and presses the submit button. This
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              makes curl pass the data to the server using the content-type application/x-www-form-urlencoded. Compare to [4m-F,[24m [4m--form[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [4m--data-raw[24m is almost the same but does not have a special interpretation of the @ character. To post data purely binary, you should instead use the [4m--data-binary[24m option. To URL-encode
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              the value of a form field you may use [4m--data-urlencode[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If any of these options is used more than once on the same command line, the data pieces specified are merged with a separating &-symbol. Thus, using '-d name=daniel -d skill=lousy'
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              would generate a post chunk that looks like 'name=daniel&skill=lousy'.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If you start the data with the letter @, the rest should be a file name to read the data from, or - if you want curl to read the data from stdin. Posting data from a file named 'foobar'
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              would thus be done with [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m @foobar. When [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m is told to read from a file like that, carriage returns and newlines are stripped out. If you do not want the @ character to
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              have a special interpretation use [4m--data-raw[24m instead.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The data for this option is passed on to the server exactly as provided on the command line. curl does not convert, change or improve it. It is up to the user to provide the data in the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              correct form.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --data can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -d "name=curl" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -d "name=curl" -d "tool=cmdline" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -d @filename https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--data-binary[24m, [4m--data-urlencode[24m and [4m--data-raw[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m-F,[24m [4m--form[24m and [4m-I,[24m [4m--head[24m and [4m-T,[24m [4m--upload-file[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --delegation <LEVEL>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (GSS/kerberos) Set LEVEL to tell the server what it is allowed to delegate when it comes to user credentials.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              none   Do not allow any delegation.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              policy Delegates if and only if the OK-AS-DELEGATE flag is set in the Kerberos service ticket, which is a matter of realm policy.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              always Unconditionally allow the server to delegate.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --delegation is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --delegation "none" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-k,[24m [4m--insecure[24m and [4m--ssl[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --digest
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Enables HTTP Digest authentication. This is an authentication scheme that prevents the password from being sent over the wire in clear text. Use this in combination with the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              normal [4m-u,[24m [4m--user[24m option to set user name and password.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --digest multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-digest.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -u name:password --digest https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-u,[24m [4m--user[24m, [4m--proxy-digest[24m and [4m--anyauth[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m--basic[24m and [4m--ntlm[24m and [4m--negotiate[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --disable-eprt
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Tell curl to disable the use of the EPRT and LPRT commands when doing active FTP transfers. Curl normally first attempts to use EPRT before using PORT, but with this option, it
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              uses PORT right away. EPRT is an extension to the original FTP protocol, and does not work on all servers, but enables more functionality in a better way than the traditional PORT
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              command.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --eprt can be used to explicitly enable EPRT again and --no-eprt is an alias for [4m--disable-eprt[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If the server is accessed using IPv6, this option has no effect as EPRT is necessary then.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Disabling EPRT only changes the active behavior. If you want to switch to passive mode you need to not use [4m-P,[24m [4m--ftp-port[24m or force it with [4m--ftp-pasv[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --disable-eprt multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-disable-eprt.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --disable-eprt ftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--disable-epsv[24m and [4m-P,[24m [4m--ftp-port[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --disable-epsv
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Tell curl to disable the use of the EPSV command when doing passive FTP transfers. Curl normally first attempts to use EPSV before PASV, but with this option, it does not try EPSV.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --epsv can be used to explicitly enable EPSV again and --no-epsv is an alias for [4m--disable-epsv[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If the server is an IPv6 host, this option has no effect as EPSV is necessary then.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Disabling EPSV only changes the passive behavior. If you want to switch to active mode you need to use [4m-P,[24m [4m--ftp-port[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --disable-epsv multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-disable-epsv.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --disable-epsv ftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--disable-eprt[24m and [4m-P,[24m [4m--ftp-port[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -q, --disable
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If used as the [1mfirst[0m parameter on the command line, the [4mcurlrc[24m config file is not read or used. See the [4m-K,[24m [4m--config[24m for details on the default config file search path.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Prior to 7.50.0 curl supported the short option name [4mq[24m but not the long option name [4mdisable[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --disable multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-disable.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -q https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-K,[24m [4m--config[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --disallow-username-in-url
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This tells curl to exit if passed a URL containing a username. This is probably most useful when the URL is being provided at runtime or similar.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --disallow-username-in-url multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-disallow-username-in-url.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --disallow-username-in-url https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--proto[24m. Added in 7.61.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --dns-interface <interface>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (DNS) Tell curl to send outgoing DNS requests through <interface>. This option is a counterpart to [4m--interface[24m (which does not affect DNS). The supplied string must be an interface name
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (not an address).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --dns-interface is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --dns-interface eth0 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--dns-ipv4-addr[24m and [4m--dns-ipv6-addr[24m. [4m--dns-interface[24m requires that the underlying libcurl was built to support c-ares.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --dns-ipv4-addr <address>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (DNS) Tell curl to bind to a specific IP address when making IPv4 DNS requests, so that the DNS requests originate from this address. The argument should be a single IPv4 address.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --dns-ipv4-addr is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --dns-ipv4-addr 10.1.2.3 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--dns-interface[24m and [4m--dns-ipv6-addr[24m. [4m--dns-ipv4-addr[24m requires that the underlying libcurl was built to support c-ares.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --dns-ipv6-addr <address>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (DNS) Tell curl to bind to a specific IP address when making IPv6 DNS requests, so that the DNS requests originate from this address. The argument should be a single IPv6 address.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --dns-ipv6-addr is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --dns-ipv6-addr 2a04:4e42::561 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--dns-interface[24m and [4m--dns-ipv4-addr[24m. [4m--dns-ipv6-addr[24m requires that the underlying libcurl was built to support c-ares.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --dns-servers <addresses>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (DNS) Set the list of DNS servers to be used instead of the system default.  The list of IP addresses should be separated with commas. Port numbers may also optionally be given as
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [4m:<port-number>[24m after each IP address.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --dns-servers is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --dns-servers 192.168.0.1,192.168.0.2 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--dns-interface[24m and [4m--dns-ipv4-addr[24m. [4m--dns-servers[24m requires that the underlying libcurl was built to support c-ares.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --doh-cert-status
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Same as [4m--cert-status[24m but used for DoH (DNS-over-HTTPS).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --doh-cert-status multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-doh-cert-status.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --doh-cert-status --doh-url https://doh.example https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--doh-insecure[24m. Added in 7.76.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --doh-insecure
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Same as [4m-k,[24m [4m--insecure[24m but used for DoH (DNS-over-HTTPS).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --doh-insecure multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-doh-insecure.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --doh-insecure --doh-url https://doh.example https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--doh-url[24m. Added in 7.76.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --doh-url <URL>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Specifies which DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH) server to use to resolve hostnames, instead of using the default name resolver mechanism. The URL must be HTTPS.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Some SSL options that you set for your transfer also applies to DoH since the name lookups take place over SSL. However, the certificate verification settings are not inherited but are
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              controlled separately via [4m--doh-insecure[24m and [4m--doh-cert-status[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option is unset if an empty string "" is used as the URL.  (Added in 7.85.0)
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --doh-url is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --doh-url https://doh.example https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--doh-insecure[24m. Added in 7.62.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -D, --dump-header <filename>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP FTP) Write the received protocol headers to the specified file. If no headers are received, the use of this option creates an empty file.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              When used in FTP, the FTP server response lines are considered being "headers" and thus are saved there.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Having multiple transfers in one set of operations (i.e. the URLs in one [4m-:,[24m [4m--next[24m clause), appends them to the same file, separated by a blank line.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --dump-header is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --dump-header store.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-o,[24m [4m--output[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --egd-file <file>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Deprecated option (added in 7.84.0). Prior to that it only had an effect on curl if built to use old versions of OpenSSL.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Specify the path name to the Entropy Gathering Daemon socket. The socket is used to seed the random engine for SSL connections.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --egd-file is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --egd-file /random/here https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--random-file[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --engine <name>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Select the OpenSSL crypto engine to use for cipher operations. Use [4m--engine[24m list to print a list of build-time supported engines. Note that not all (and possibly none) of the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              engines may be available at runtime.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --engine is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --engine flavor https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ciphers[24m and [4m--curves[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --etag-compare <file>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) This option makes a conditional HTTP request for the specific ETag read from the given file by sending a custom If-None-Match header using the stored ETag.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For correct results, make sure that the specified file contains only a single line with the desired ETag. An empty file is parsed as an empty ETag.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Use the option [4m--etag-save[24m to first save the ETag from a response, and then use this option to compare against the saved ETag in a subsequent request.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --etag-compare is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --etag-compare etag.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--etag-save[24m and [4m-z,[24m [4m--time-cond[24m. Added in 7.68.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --etag-save <file>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) This option saves an HTTP ETag to the specified file. An ETag is a caching related header, usually returned in a response.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If no ETag is sent by the server, an empty file is created.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --etag-save is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --etag-save storetag.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
