:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--etag-compare[24m. Added in 7.68.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --expect100-timeout <seconds>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Maximum time in seconds that you allow curl to wait for a 100-continue response when curl emits an Expects: 100-continue header in its request. By default curl waits one second.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option accepts decimal values! When curl stops waiting, it continues as if the response has been received.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The decimal value needs to provided using a dot (.) as decimal separator - not the local version even if it might be using another separator.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --expect100-timeout is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --expect100-timeout 2.5 -T file https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--connect-timeout[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --fail-early
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Fail and exit on the first detected transfer error.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              When curl is used to do multiple transfers on the command line, it attempts to operate on each given URL, one by one. By default, it ignores errors if there are more URLs given and the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              last URL's success determines the error code curl returns. So early failures are "hidden" by subsequent successful transfers.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Using this option, curl instead returns an error on the first transfer that fails, independent of the amount of URLs that are given on the command line. This way, no transfer failures go
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              undetected by scripts and similar.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option does not imply [4m-f,[24m [4m--fail[24m, which causes transfers to fail due to the server's HTTP status code. You can combine the two options, however note [4m-f,[24m [4m--fail[24m is not global and is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              therefore contained by [4m-:,[24m [4m--next[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option is global and does not need to be specified for each use of --next.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --fail-early multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-fail-early.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --fail-early https://example.com https://two.example
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-f,[24m [4m--fail[24m and [4m--fail-with-body[24m. Added in 7.52.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --fail-with-body
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Return an error on server errors where the HTTP response code is 400 or greater). In normal cases when an HTTP server fails to deliver a document, it returns an HTML document
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              stating so (which often also describes why and more). This flag allows curl to output and save that content but also to return error 22.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This is an alternative option to [4m-f,[24m [4m--fail[24m which makes curl fail for the same circumstances but without saving the content.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --fail-with-body multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-fail-with-body.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --fail-with-body https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-f,[24m [4m--fail[24m and [4m--fail-early[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m-f,[24m [4m--fail[24m. Added in 7.76.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -f, --fail
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Fail fast with no output at all on server errors. This is useful to enable scripts and users to better deal with failed attempts. In normal cases when an HTTP server fails to
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              deliver a document, it returns an HTML document stating so (which often also describes why and more). This flag prevents curl from outputting that and return error 22.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This method is not fail-safe and there are occasions where non-successful response codes slip through, especially when authentication is involved (response codes 401 and 407).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --fail multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-fail.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --fail https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--fail-with-body[24m and [4m--fail-early[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m--fail-with-body[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --false-start
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Tells curl to use false start during the TLS handshake. False start is a mode where a TLS client starts sending application data before verifying the server's Finished message,
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              thus saving a round trip when performing a full handshake.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This is currently only implemented in the Secure Transport (on iOS 7.0 or later, or OS X 10.9 or later) backend.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --false-start multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-false-start.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --false-start https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--tcp-fastopen[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --form-escape
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Tells curl to pass on names of multipart form fields and files using backslash-escaping instead of percent-encoding.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --form-escape is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --form-escape -F 'field\name=curl' -F 'file=@load"this' https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-F,[24m [4m--form[24m. Added in 7.81.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --form-string <name=string>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP SMTP IMAP) Similar to [4m-F,[24m [4m--form[24m except that the value string for the named parameter is used literally. Leading '@' and '<' characters, and the ';type=' string in the value have
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              no special meaning. Use this in preference to [4m-F,[24m [4m--form[24m if there is any possibility that the string value may accidentally trigger the '@' or '<' features of [4m-F,[24m [4m--form[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --form-string can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --form-string "data" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-F,[24m [4m--form[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -F, --form <name=content>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP SMTP IMAP) For HTTP protocol family, this lets curl emulate a filled-in form in which a user has pressed the submit button. This causes curl to POST data using the Content-Type
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              multipart/form-data according to RFC 2388.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For SMTP and IMAP protocols, this is the means to compose a multipart mail message to transmit.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This enables uploading of binary files etc. To force the 'content' part to be a file, prefix the file name with an @ sign. To just get the content part from a file, prefix the file name
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              with the symbol <. The difference between @ and < is then that @ makes a file get attached in the post as a file upload, while the < makes a text field and just get the contents for that
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              text field from a file.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Tell curl to read content from stdin instead of a file by using - as filename. This goes for both @ and < constructs. When stdin is used, the contents is buffered in memory first by curl
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              to determine its size and allow a possible resend. Defining a part's data from a named non-regular file (such as a named pipe or similar) is not subject to buffering and is instead read
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              at transmission time; since the full size is unknown before the transfer starts, such data is sent as chunks by HTTP and rejected by IMAP.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example: send an image to an HTTP server, where 'profile' is the name of the form-field to which the file [1mportrait.jpg[0m is the input:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F profile=@portrait.jpg https://example.com/upload.cgi
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example: send your name and shoe size in two text fields to the server:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F name=John -F shoesize=11 https://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example: send your essay in a text field to the server. Send it as a plain text field, but get the contents for it from a local file:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F "story=<hugefile.txt" https://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              You can also tell curl what Content-Type to use by using 'type=', in a manner similar to:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F "web=@index.html;type=text/html" example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              or
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F "name=daniel;type=text/foo" example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              You can also explicitly change the name field of a file upload part by setting filename=, like this:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F "file=@localfile;filename=nameinpost" example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If filename/path contains ',' or ';', it must be quoted by double-quotes like:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F "file=@\"local,file\";filename=\"name;in;post\"" example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              or
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F 'file=@"local,file";filename="name;in;post"' example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Note that if a filename/path is quoted by double-quotes, any double-quote or backslash within the filename must be escaped by backslash.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Quoting must also be applied to non-file data if it contains semicolons, leading/trailing spaces or leading double quotes:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F 'colors="red; green; blue";type=text/x-myapp' example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              You can add custom headers to the field by setting headers=, like
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F "submit=OK;headers=\"X-submit-type: OK\"" example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              or
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F "submit=OK;headers=@headerfile" example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The headers= keyword may appear more that once and above notes about quoting apply. When headers are read from a file, Empty lines and lines starting with '#' are comments and ignored;
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              each header can be folded by splitting between two words and starting the continuation line with a space; embedded carriage-returns and trailing spaces are stripped.  Here is an example
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              of a header file contents:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              # This file contain two headers.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              X-header-1: this is a header
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              # The following header is folded.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              X-header-2: this is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               another header
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              To support sending multipart mail messages, the syntax is extended as follows:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              - name can be omitted: the equal sign is the first character of the argument,
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              - if data starts with '(', this signals to start a new multipart: it can be followed by a content type specification.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              - a multipart can be terminated with a '=)' argument.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example: the following command sends an SMTP mime email consisting in an inline part in two alternative formats: plain text and HTML. It attaches a text file:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F '=(;type=multipart/alternative' \
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                   -F '=plain text message' \
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                   -F '= <body>HTML message</body>;type=text/html' \
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                   -F '=)' -F '=@textfile.txt' ...  smtp://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Data can be encoded for transfer using encoder=. Available encodings are [4mbinary[24m and [4m8bit[24m that do nothing else than adding the corresponding Content-Transfer-Encoding header, [4m7bit[24m that
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              only rejects 8-bit characters with a transfer error, [4mquoted-printable[24m and [4mbase64[24m that encodes data according to the corresponding schemes, limiting lines length to 76 characters.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example: send multipart mail with a quoted-printable text message and a base64 attached file:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl -F '=text message;encoder=quoted-printable' \
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                   -F '=@localfile;encoder=base64' ... smtp://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See further examples and details in the MANUAL.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --form can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --form "name=curl" --form "file=@loadthis" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m, [4m--form-string[24m and [4m--form-escape[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m and [4m-I,[24m [4m--head[24m and [4m-T,[24m [4m--upload-file[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-account <data>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) When an FTP server asks for "account data" after user name and password has been provided, this data is sent off using the ACCT command.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --ftp-account is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-account "mr.robot" ftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-u,[24m [4m--user[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-alternative-to-user <command>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) If authenticating with the USER and PASS commands fails, send this command.  When connecting to Tumbleweed's Secure Transport server over FTPS using a client certificate, using
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              "SITE AUTH" tells the server to retrieve the username from the certificate.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --ftp-alternative-to-user is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-alternative-to-user "U53r" ftp://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ftp-account[24m and [4m-u,[24m [4m--user[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-create-dirs
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP SFTP) When an FTP or SFTP URL/operation uses a path that does not currently exist on the server, the standard behavior of curl is to fail. Using this option, curl instead attempts
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              to create missing directories.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ftp-create-dirs multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-ftp-create-dirs.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-create-dirs -T file ftp://example.com/remote/path/file
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--create-dirs[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-method <method>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Control what method curl should use to reach a file on an FTP(S) server. The method argument should be one of the following alternatives:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              multicwd
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     curl does a single CWD operation for each path part in the given URL. For deep hierarchies this means many commands. This is how RFC 1738 says it should be done. This is the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     default but the slowest behavior.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              nocwd  curl does no CWD at all. curl does SIZE, RETR, STOR etc and give a full path to the server for all these commands. This is the fastest behavior.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              singlecwd
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     curl does one CWD with the full target directory and then operates on the file "normally" (like in the multicwd case). This is somewhat more standards compliant than 'nocwd' but
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     without the full penalty of 'multicwd'.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --ftp-method is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-method multicwd ftp://example.com/dir1/dir2/file
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-method nocwd ftp://example.com/dir1/dir2/file
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-method singlecwd ftp://example.com/dir1/dir2/file
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-l,[24m [4m--list-only[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-pasv
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Use passive mode for the data connection. Passive is the internal default behavior, but using this option can be used to override a previous [4m-P,[24m [4m--ftp-port[24m option.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Reversing an enforced passive really is not doable but you must then instead enforce the correct [4m-P,[24m [4m--ftp-port[24m again.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Passive mode means that curl tries the EPSV command first and then PASV, unless [4m--disable-epsv[24m is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ftp-pasv multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-ftp-pasv.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-pasv ftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--disable-epsv[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -P, --ftp-port <address>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Reverses the default initiator/listener roles when connecting with FTP. This option makes curl use active mode. curl then tells the server to connect back to the client's specified
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              address and port, while passive mode asks the server to setup an IP address and port for it to connect to. <address> should be one of:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              interface
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     e.g. [1meth0[0m to specify which interface's IP address you want to use (Unix only)
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              IP address
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     e.g. [1m192.168.10.1[0m to specify the exact IP address
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              host name
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K                     e.g. [1mmy.host.domain[0m to specify the machine
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              -      make curl pick the same IP address that is already used for the control connection. This is the recommended choice.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Disable the use of PORT with [4m--ftp-pasv[24m. Disable the attempt to use the EPRT command instead of PORT by using [4m--disable-eprt[24m. EPRT is really PORT++.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              You can also append ":[start]-[end]" to the right of the address, to tell curl what TCP port range to use. That means you specify a port range, from a lower to a higher number. A single
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              number works as well, but do note that it increases the risk of failure since the port may not be available.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --ftp-port is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -P - ftp:/example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -P eth0 ftp:/example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -P 192.168.0.2 ftp:/example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ftp-pasv[24m and [4m--disable-eprt[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-pret
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Tell curl to send a PRET command before PASV (and EPSV). Certain FTP servers, mainly drftpd, require this non-standard command for directory listings as well as up and downloads in
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              PASV mode.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ftp-pret multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-ftp-pret.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-pret ftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-P,[24m [4m--ftp-port[24m and [4m--ftp-pasv[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-skip-pasv-ip
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Tell curl to not use the IP address the server suggests in its response to curl's PASV command when curl connects the data connection. Instead curl reuses the same IP address it
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              already uses for the control connection.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option is enabled by default (added in 7.74.0).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option has no effect if PORT, EPRT or EPSV is used instead of PASV.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ftp-skip-pasv-ip multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-ftp-skip-pasv-ip.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-skip-pasv-ip ftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ftp-pasv[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-ssl-ccc-mode <active/passive>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Sets the CCC mode. The passive mode does not initiate the shutdown, but instead waits for the server to do it, and does not reply to the shutdown from the server. The active mode
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              initiates the shutdown and waits for a reply from the server.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ftp-ssl-ccc-mode multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-ftp-ssl-ccc-mode.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-ssl-ccc-mode active --ftp-ssl-ccc ftps://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ftp-ssl-ccc[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-ssl-ccc
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Use CCC (Clear Command Channel) Shuts down the SSL/TLS layer after authenticating. The rest of the control channel communication is be unencrypted. This allows NAT routers to
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              follow the FTP transaction. The default mode is passive.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ftp-ssl-ccc multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-ftp-ssl-ccc.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-ssl-ccc ftps://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ssl[24m and [4m--ftp-ssl-ccc-mode[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ftp-ssl-control
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Require SSL/TLS for the FTP login, clear for transfer. Allows secure authentication, but non-encrypted data transfers for efficiency. Fails the transfer if the server does not
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              support SSL/TLS.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ftp-ssl-control multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-ftp-ssl-control.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ftp-ssl-control ftp://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ssl[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -G, --get
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) When used, this option makes all data specified with [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m, [4m--data-binary[24m or [4m--data-urlencode[24m to be used in an HTTP GET request instead of the POST request that otherwise
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              would be used. The data is appended to the URL with a '?' separator.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If used in combination with [4m-I,[24m [4m--head[24m, the POST data is instead appended to the URL with a HEAD request.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --get multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-get.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --get https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --get -d "tool=curl" -d "age=old" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --get -I -d "tool=curl" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-d,[24m [4m--data[24m and [4m-X,[24m [4m--request[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -g, --globoff
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option switches off the "URL globbing parser". When you set this option, you can specify URLs that contain the letters {}[] without having curl itself interpret them. Note that
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              these letters are not normal legal URL contents but they should be encoded according to the URI standard.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --globoff multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-globoff.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -g "https://example.com/{[]}}}}"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-K,[24m [4m--config[24m and [4m-q,[24m [4m--disable[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --happy-eyeballs-timeout-ms <milliseconds>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Happy Eyeballs is an algorithm that attempts to connect to both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses for dual-stack hosts, giving IPv6 a head-start of the specified number of milliseconds. If the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              IPv6 address cannot be connected to within that time, then a connection attempt is made to the IPv4 address in parallel. The first connection to be established is the one that is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The range of suggested useful values is limited. Happy Eyeballs RFC 6555 says "It is RECOMMENDED that connection attempts be paced 150-250 ms apart to balance human factors against
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              network load." libcurl currently defaults to 200 ms. Firefox and Chrome currently default to 300 ms.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --happy-eyeballs-timeout-ms is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --happy-eyeballs-timeout-ms 500 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-m,[24m [4m--max-time[24m and [4m--connect-timeout[24m. Added in 7.59.0.
[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --haproxy-clientip <IP address>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Sets a client IP in HAProxy PROXY protocol v1 header at the beginning of the connection.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For valid requests, IPv4 addresses must be indicated as a series of exactly 4 integers in the range [0..255] inclusive written in decimal representation separated by exactly one dot
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              between each other. Heading zeroes are not permitted in front of numbers in order to avoid any possible confusion with octal numbers. IPv6 addresses must be indicated as series of 4
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              hexadecimal digits (upper or lower case) delimited by colons between each other, with the acceptance of one double colon sequence to replace the largest acceptable range of consecutive
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              zeroes. The total number of decoded bits must exactly be 128.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Otherwise, any string can be accepted for the client IP and get sent.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              It replaces [4m--haproxy-protocol[24m if used, it is not necessary to specify both flags.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --haproxy-clientip is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --haproxy-clientip $IP
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-x,[24m [4m--proxy[24m. Added in 8.2.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --haproxy-protocol
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Send a HAProxy PROXY protocol v1 header at the beginning of the connection. This is used by some load balancers and reverse proxies to indicate the client's true IP address and
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              port.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option is primarily useful when sending test requests to a service that expects this header.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --haproxy-protocol multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-haproxy-protocol.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --haproxy-protocol https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-x,[24m [4m--proxy[24m. Added in 7.60.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -I, --head
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP FTP FILE) Fetch the headers only! HTTP-servers feature the command HEAD which this uses to get nothing but the header of a document. When used on an FTP or FILE file, curl displays
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              the file size and last modification time only.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --head multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-head.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -I https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-G,[24m [4m--get[24m, [4m-v,[24m [4m--verbose[24m and [4m--trace-ascii[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -H, --header <header/@file>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP IMAP SMTP) Extra header to include in information sent. When used within an HTTP request, it is added to the regular request headers.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For an IMAP or SMTP MIME uploaded mail built with [4m-F,[24m [4m--form[24m options, it is prepended to the resulting MIME document, effectively including it at the mail global level. It does not
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              affect raw uploaded mails (Added in 7.56.0).
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              You may specify any number of extra headers. Note that if you should add a custom header that has the same name as one of the internal ones curl would use, your externally set header is
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              used instead of the internal one. This allows you to make even trickier stuff than curl would normally do. You should not replace internally set headers without knowing perfectly well
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              what you are doing. Remove an internal header by giving a replacement without content on the right side of the colon, as in: -H "Host:". If you send the custom header with no-value then
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              its header must be terminated with a semicolon, such as \-H "X-Custom-Header;" to send "X-Custom-Header:".
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl makes sure that each header you add/replace is sent with the proper end-of-line marker, you should thus [1mnot[0m add that as a part of the header content: do not add newlines or carriage
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              returns, they only mess things up for you. curl passes on the verbatim string you give it without any filter or other safe guards. That includes white space and control characters.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option can take an argument in @filename style, which then adds a header for each line in the input file. Using @- makes curl read the header file from stdin. Added in 7.55.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Please note that most anti-spam utilities check the presence and value of several MIME mail headers: these are "From:", "To:", "Date:" and "Subject:" among others and should be added
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              with this option.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              You need [4m--proxy-header[24m to send custom headers intended for an HTTP proxy. Added in 7.37.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Passing on a "Transfer-Encoding: chunked" header when doing an HTTP request with a request body, makes curl send the data using chunked encoding.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [1mWARNING[0m: headers set with this option are set in all HTTP requests - even after redirects are followed, like when told with [4m-L,[24m [4m--location[24m. This can lead to the header being sent to
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              other hosts than the original host, so sensitive headers should be used with caution combined with following redirects.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --header can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -H "X-First-Name: Joe" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -H "User-Agent: yes-please/2000" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -H "Host:" https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -H @headers.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-A,[24m [4m--user-agent[24m and [4m-e,[24m [4m--referer[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -h, --help <category>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Usage help. This lists all curl command line options within the given [1mcategory[0m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If no argument is provided, curl displays only the most important command line arguments.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For category [1mall[0m, curl displays help for all options.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If [1mcategory[0m is specified, curl displays all available help categories.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --help all
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-v,[24m [4m--verbose[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --hostpubmd5 <md5>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (SFTP SCP) Pass a string containing 32 hexadecimal digits. The string should be the 128 bit [1mMD5[0m checksum of the remote host's public key, curl refuses the connection with the host unless
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              the checksums match.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --hostpubmd5 is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --hostpubmd5 e5c1c49020640a5ab0f2034854c321a8 sftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--hostpubsha256[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --hostpubsha256 <sha256>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (SFTP SCP) Pass a string containing a Base64-encoded SHA256 hash of the remote host's public key. Curl refuses the connection with the host unless the hashes match.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This feature requires libcurl to be built with libssh2 and does not work with other SSH backends.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --hostpubsha256 is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --hostpubsha256 NDVkMTQxMGQ1ODdmMjQ3MjczYjAyOTY5MmRkMjVmNDQ= sftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--hostpubmd5[24m. Added in 7.80.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --hsts <file name>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTPS) This option enables HSTS for the transfer. If the file name points to an existing HSTS cache file, that is used. After a completed transfer, the cache is saved to the file name
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              again if it has been modified.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If curl is told to use HTTP:// for a transfer involving a host name that exists in the HSTS cache, it upgrades the transfer to use HTTPS. Each HSTS cache entry has an individual life
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              time after which the upgrade is no longer performed.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Specify a "" file name (zero length) to avoid loading/saving and make curl just handle HSTS in memory.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If this option is used several times, curl loads contents from all the files but the last one is used for saving.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --hsts can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --hsts cache.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--proto[24m. Added in 7.74.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --http0.9
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Tells curl to be fine with HTTP version 0.9 response.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              HTTP/0.9 is a response without headers and therefore you can also connect with this to non-HTTP servers and still get a response since curl simply transparently downgrades - if allowed.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              HTTP/0.9 is disabled by default (added in 7.66.0)
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --http0.9 multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-http0.9.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --http0.9 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--http1.1[24m, [4m--http2[24m and [4m--http3[24m. Added in 7.64.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -0, --http1.0
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Tells curl to use HTTP version 1.0 instead of using its internally preferred HTTP version.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --http1.0 multiple times has no extra effect.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --http1.0 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--http0.9[24m and [4m--http1.1[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m--http1.1[24m and [4m--http2[24m and [4m--http2-prior-knowledge[24m and [4m--http3[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --http1.1
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Tells curl to use HTTP version 1.1.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --http1.1 multiple times has no extra effect.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --http1.1 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-0,[24m [4m--http1.0[24m and [4m--http0.9[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m-0,[24m [4m--http1.0[24m and [4m--http2[24m and [4m--http2-prior-knowledge[24m and [4m--http3[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --http2-prior-knowledge
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Tells curl to issue its non-TLS HTTP requests using HTTP/2 without HTTP/1.1 Upgrade. It requires prior knowledge that the server supports HTTP/2 straight away. HTTPS requests
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              still do HTTP/2 the standard way with negotiated protocol version in the TLS handshake.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --http2-prior-knowledge multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-http2-prior-knowledge.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --http2-prior-knowledge https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--http2[24m and [4m--http3[24m. [4m--http2-prior-knowledge[24m requires that the underlying libcurl was built to support HTTP/2. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m--http1.1[24m and [4m-0,[24m [4m--http1.0[24m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              and [4m--http2[24m and [4m--http3[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --http2
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Tells curl to use HTTP version 2.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For HTTPS, this means curl negotiates HTTP/2 in the TLS handshake. curl does this by default.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For HTTP, this means curl attempts to upgrade the request to HTTP/2 using the Upgrade: request header.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              When curl uses HTTP/2 over HTTPS, it does not itself insist on TLS 1.2 or higher even though that is required by the specification. A user can add this version requirement with
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [4m--tlsv1.2[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --http2 multiple times has no extra effect.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --http2 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--http1.1[24m, [4m--http3[24m and [4m--no-alpn[24m. [4m--http2[24m requires that the underlying libcurl was built to support HTTP/2. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m--http1.1[24m and [4m-0,[24m [4m--http1.0[24m and
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [4m--http2-prior-knowledge[24m and [4m--http3[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --http3-only
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Instructs curl to use HTTP/3 to the host in the URL, with no fallback to earlier HTTP versions. HTTP/3 can only be used for HTTPS and not for HTTP URLs. For HTTP, this option
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              triggers an error.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option allows a user to avoid using the Alt-Svc method of upgrading to HTTP/3 when you know that the target speaks HTTP/3 on the given host and port.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option makes curl fail if a QUIC connection cannot be established, it does not attempt any other HTTP versions on its own. Use [4m--http3[24m for similar functionality [4mwith[24m a fallback.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --http3-only multiple times has no extra effect.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --http3-only https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--http1.1[24m, [4m--http2[24m and [4m--http3[24m. [4m--http3-only[24m requires that the underlying libcurl was built to support HTTP/3. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m--http1.1[24m and [4m-0,[24m [4m--http1.0[24m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              and [4m--http2[24m and [4m--http2-prior-knowledge[24m and [4m--http3[24m. Added in 7.88.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --http3
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Tells curl to try HTTP/3 to the host in the URL, but fallback to earlier HTTP versions if the HTTP/3 connection establishment fails. HTTP/3 is only available for HTTPS and not for
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              HTTP URLs.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option allows a user to avoid using the Alt-Svc method of upgrading to HTTP/3 when you know that the target speaks HTTP/3 on the given host and port.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              When asked to use HTTP/3, curl issues a separate attempt to use older HTTP versions with a slight delay, so if the HTTP/3 transfer fails or is slow, curl still tries to proceed with an
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              older HTTP version.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Use [4m--http3-only[24m for similar functionality [4mwithout[24m a fallback.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --http3 multiple times has no extra effect.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --http3 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--http1.1[24m and [4m--http2[24m. [4m--http3[24m requires that the underlying libcurl was built to support HTTP/3. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m--http1.1[24m and [4m-0,[24m [4m--http1.0[24m and [4m--http2[24m and
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [4m--http2-prior-knowledge[24m and [4m--http3-only[24m. Added in 7.66.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ignore-content-length
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP HTTP) For HTTP, Ignore the Content-Length header. This is particularly useful for servers running Apache 1.x, which reports incorrect Content-Length for files larger than 2
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              gigabytes.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For FTP, this makes curl skip the SIZE command to figure out the size before downloading a file.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option does not work for HTTP if libcurl was built to use hyper.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ignore-content-length multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-ignore-content-length.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ignore-content-length https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--ftp-skip-pasv-ip[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -i, --include
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP FTP) Include response headers in the output. HTTP response headers can include things like server name, cookies, date of the document, HTTP version and more... With non-HTTP
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              protocols, the "headers" are other server communication.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              To view the request headers, consider the [4m-v,[24m [4m--verbose[24m option.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Prior to 7.75.0 curl did not print the headers if [4m-f,[24m [4m--fail[24m was used in combination with this option and there was error reported by server.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --include multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-include.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl -i https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-v,[24m [4m--verbose[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -k, --insecure
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS SFTP SCP) By default, every secure connection curl makes is verified to be secure before the transfer takes place. This option makes curl skip the verification step and proceed
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              without checking.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              When this option is not used for protocols using TLS, curl verifies the server's TLS certificate before it continues: that the certificate contains the right name which matches the host
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              name used in the URL and that the certificate has been signed by a CA certificate present in the cert store.  See this online resource for further details:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [1mhttps://curl.se/docs/sslcerts.html[0m
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              For SFTP and SCP, this option makes curl skip the [4mknown_hosts[24m verification.  [4mknown_hosts[24m is a file normally stored in the user's home directory in the ".ssh" subdirectory, which contains
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              host names and their public keys.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              [1mWARNING[0m: using this option makes the transfer insecure.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              When curl uses secure protocols it trusts responses and allows for example HSTS and Alt-Svc information to be stored and used subsequently. Using [4m-k,[24m [4m--insecure[24m can make curl trust and
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              use such information from malicious servers.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --insecure multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-insecure.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --insecure https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--proxy-insecure[24m, [4m--cacert[24m and [4m--capath[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --interface <name>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Perform an operation using a specified interface. You can enter interface name, IP address or host name. An example could look like:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl --interface eth0:1 https://www.example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              On Linux it can be used to specify a [1mVRF[0m, but the binary needs to either have [1mCAP_NET_RAW[0m or to be run as root. More information about Linux [1mVRF[0m:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/networking/vrf.txt
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --interface is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --interface eth0 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--dns-interface[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --ipfs-gateway <URL>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (IPFS) Specify which gateway to use for IPFS and IPNS URLs. Not specifying this will instead make curl check if the IPFS_GATEWAY environment variable is set, or if a "~/.ipfs/gateway"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              file holding the gateway URL exists.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If you run a local IPFS node, this gateway is by default available under "http://localhost:8080". A full example URL would look like:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl --ipfs-gateway http://localhost:8080 ipfs://bafybeigagd5nmnn2iys2f3doro7ydrevyr2mzarwidgadawmamiteydbzi
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              There are many public IPFS gateways. See for example: https://ipfs.github.io/public-gateway-checker/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              WARNING: If you opt to go for a remote gateway you should be aware that you completely trust the gateway. This is fine in local gateways as you host it yourself. With remote gateways
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              there could potentially be a malicious actor returning you data that does not match the request you made, inspect or even interfere with the request. You will not notice this when using
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              curl. A mitigation could be to go for a "trustless" gateway. This means you locally verify that the data. Consult the docs page on trusted vs trustless:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              https://docs.ipfs.tech/reference/http/gateway/#trusted-vs-trustless
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --ipfs-gateway is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ipfs-gateway https://example.com ipfs://
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-h,[24m [4m--help[24m and [4m-M,[24m [4m--manual[24m. Added in 8.4.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -4, --ipv4
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option tells curl to use IPv4 addresses only when resolving host names, and not for example try IPv6.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ipv4 multiple times has no extra effect.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ipv4 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--http1.1[24m and [4m--http2[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m-6,[24m [4m--ipv6[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -6, --ipv6
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option tells curl to use IPv6 addresses only when resolving host names, and not for example try IPv4.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --ipv6 multiple times has no extra effect.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --ipv6 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--http1.1[24m and [4m--http2[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m-4,[24m [4m--ipv4[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --json <data>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) Sends the specified JSON data in a POST request to the HTTP server. [4m--json[24m works as a shortcut for passing on these three options:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --data [arg]
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --header "Content-Type: application/json"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --header "Accept: application/json"
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              There is [1mno[0m [1mverification[0m that the passed in data is actual JSON or that the syntax is correct.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If you start the data with the letter @, the rest should be a file name to read the data from, or a single dash (-) if you want curl to read the data from stdin. Posting data from a file
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              named 'foobar' would thus be done with [4m--json[24m @foobar and to instead read the data from stdin, use [4m--json[24m @-.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If this option is used more than once on the same command line, the additional data pieces are concatenated to the previous before sending.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              The headers this option sets can be overridden with [4m-H,[24m [4m--header[24m as usual.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              --json can be used several times in a command line
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Examples:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --json '{ "drink": "coffe" }' https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --json '{ "drink":' --json ' "coffe" }' https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --json @prepared https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --json @- https://example.com < json.txt
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--data-binary[24m and [4m--data-raw[24m. This option is mutually exclusive to [4m-F,[24m [4m--form[24m and [4m-I,[24m [4m--head[24m and [4m-T,[24m [4m--upload-file[24m. Added in 7.82.0.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       -j, --junk-session-cookies
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (HTTP) When curl is told to read cookies from a given file, this option makes it discard all "session cookies". This has the same effect as if a new session is started. Typical browsers
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              discard session cookies when they are closed down.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Providing --junk-session-cookies multiple times has no extra effect.  Disable it again with --no-junk-session-cookies.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --junk-session-cookies -b cookies.txt https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m-b,[24m [4m--cookie[24m and [4m-c,[24m [4m--cookie-jar[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --keepalive-time <seconds>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              This option sets the time a connection needs to remain idle before sending keepalive probes and the time between individual keepalive probes. It is currently effective on operating
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              systems offering the "TCP_KEEPIDLE" and "TCP_KEEPINTVL" socket options (meaning Linux, recent AIX, HP-UX and more).  Keepalive is used by the TCP stack to detect broken networks on idle
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              connections. The number of missed keepalive probes before declaring the connection down is OS dependent and is commonly 9 or 10. This option has no effect if [4m--no-keepalive[24m is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If unspecified, the option defaults to 60 seconds.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --keepalive-time is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --keepalive-time 20 https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--no-keepalive[24m and [4m-m,[24m [4m--max-time[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --key-type <type>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS) Private key file type. Specify which type your [4m--key[24m provided private key is. DER, PEM, and ENG are supported. If not specified, PEM is assumed.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --key-type is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --key-type DER --key here https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--key[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --key <key>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (TLS SSH) Private key file name. Allows you to provide your private key in this separate file. For SSH, if not specified, curl tries the following candidates in order: "~/.ssh/id_rsa",
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              "~/.ssh/id_dsa", "./id_rsa", "./id_dsa".
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If curl is built against OpenSSL library, and the engine pkcs11 is available, then a PKCS#11 URI (RFC 7512) can be used to specify a private key located in a PKCS#11 device. A string
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              beginning with "pkcs11:" is interpreted as a PKCS#11 URI. If a PKCS#11 URI is provided, then the [4m--engine[24m option is set as "pkcs11" if none was provided and the [4m--key-type[24m option is set
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              as "ENG" if none was provided.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If curl is built against Secure Transport or Schannel then this option is ignored for TLS protocols (HTTPS, etc). Those backends expect the private key to be already present in the
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              keychain or PKCS#12 file containing the certificate.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --key is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --cert certificate --key here https://example.com
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--key-type[24m and [4m-E,[24m [4m--cert[24m.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --krb <level>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              (FTP) Enable Kerberos authentication and use. The level must be entered and should be one of 'clear', 'safe', 'confidential', or 'private'. Should you use a level that is not one of
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              these, 'private' is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              If --krb is provided several times, the last set value is used.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Example:
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K               curl --krb clear ftp://example.com/
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              See also [4m--delegation[24m and [4m--ssl[24m. [4m--krb[24m requires that the underlying libcurl was built to support Kerberos.
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K       --libcurl <file>
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K              Append this option to any ordinary curl command line, and you get libcurl-using C source code written to the file that does the equivalent of what your command-line operation does!
:[K[K [KESCESC[KOO[KBB[K
